response.justification: The candidate is a clearly experienced, versatile senior engineer with genuine language-tooling interest (D test runners, assertion libraries) and direct IDE development experience at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI). However, the role's core required stack — strong Java and Kotlin expertise, deep JVM understanding, and solid Spring ecosystem experience — is essentially absent: Java appears only as a secondary skill in older roles, and Kotlin and Spring do not appear at all. His recent decade centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, making him a weak fit for an enterprise JVM tooling position despite strong general engineering craft.
strengths: Prior IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Demonstrated interest in language tooling and developer experience (built D test runner, assertion library, web server)
Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, testing workshops, large-scale low-latency systems)
concerns: No Kotlin and no Spring/enterprise-framework experience anywhere on the resume
Java exposure is minimal, dated, and peripheral — no evidence of deep JVM understanding
Recent experience is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js) rather than JVM tooling or enterprise frameworks
